article_title: Surgical management of adverse events associated with deep brain stimulation: A single-center experience
label: Figure 2.
caption: An axial MRI image showing the misplaced deep brain stimulation (DBS) lead tip on the left side of the brain: (a) It is located posterolaterally to the ideal location (arrow); (b) the left DBS lead was repositioned to the globus pallidus interna (GPi).

article_title: Surgical management of adverse events associated with deep brain stimulation: A single-center experience
label: Figure 3.
caption: X-ray films showing an additional deep brain stimulation (DBS) lead in the globus pallidus interna (GPi) (a) and the replaced dual-channel implantable pulse generator (b).

article_title: Surgical management of adverse events associated with deep brain stimulation: A single-center experience
label: Figure 4.
caption: A ventrally migrated deep brain stimulation (DBS) lead is shown on the coronal (a) and axial (b, c) CT images and a skull x-ray (d). Two preoperative axial CT images (b, c) show the tips of the left and right DBS leads, respectively. The left and right leads were replaced with a new lead in a staged fashion (e, f).

article_title: Recurrent, Delayed Hemorrhage Associated with Edoxaban after Deep Brain Stimulation Lead Placement
label: Figure 1
caption: (a) Axial CT without contrast enhancement showing hypodense 1.5 cm ovoid lesion in left basal ganglia surrounding deep brain stimulator lead four months after DBS placement. (b) The lesion appears hyperintense on axial T2-weighted MRI.

article_title: Recurrent, Delayed Hemorrhage Associated with Edoxaban after Deep Brain Stimulation Lead Placement
label: Figure 2
caption: Axial CT without contrast enhancement showing no acute hemorrhage along DBS tract immediately after DBS placement.

article_title: Recurrent, Delayed Hemorrhage Associated with Edoxaban after Deep Brain Stimulation Lead Placement
label: Figure 3
caption: Axial CT without contrast enhancement showing 1.9 × 1.5 cm acute hemorrhage in the left cerebral peduncle five months after DBS placement.

article_title: Recurrent, Delayed Hemorrhage Associated with Edoxaban after Deep Brain Stimulation Lead Placement
label: Figure 4
caption: Repeat axial head CT without contrast enhancement done 24 hours after initial CT scan showing interval improvement of left cerebral peduncle hemorrhage.

article_title: Case report of hyperacute edema and cavitation following deep brain stimulation lead implantation
label: Figure 1:
caption: (a) Unremarkable head computed tomography (CT) performed early on postoperative day (POD) 1. (b) Stat head CT later on POD 1 showing marked left-sided peri-lead edema extending into the centrum semiovale with cystic cavitation and trace right-sided edema. (c) Follow-up head CT at 3-month clinic visit showing significant resolution of the peri-lead edema and cystic cavitation. The leftmost image corresponds to the top yellow line in the rightmost image, and the middle image corresponds to the bottom yellow line in the rightmost image.
